channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: The Impact of Mandatory Bundled Payment on Prehabilitation Delivery for Lower Extremity Joint Replacement.
item.description: A difference-in-differences analysis (n=62,217 Medicare beneficiaries) found that the Comprehensive Care for Joint Replacement (CJR) bundled payment model impacted the proportion of hospitals delivering prehabilitation for lower extremity joint replacement.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The effectiveness of cardiovascular rehabilitation for transient ischaemic attack and mild stroke: A randomised controlled trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial of 140 TIA/mild stroke patients found a 6-week cardiovascular rehabilitation program significantly improved 6-min walk test distance and moderate-to-vigorous physical activity over 6 months compared to usual care.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Cross-Education Effect on Force Production Capacity After Unilateral Resistance Training in Clinical Populations: A Systematic Review with Meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review with meta-analysis found the cross-education effect after unilateral resistance training (CE-URT) on force production capacity is present in clinical populations, including those with musculoskeletal conditions and hemiparesis after stroke.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Cost-Effectiveness of Post-Acute Stroke Rehabilitation From a U.S. Healthcare Payer Perspective: Inpatient Rehabilitation Facilities Versus Skilled Nursing Facilities.
item.description: A decision analytic model evaluating 90-day outcomes for stroke survivors found Inpatient Rehabilitation Facilities (IRFs) to be more cost-effective than Skilled Nursing Facilities (SNFs) from a US healthcare payer perspective.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Psychometric Properties of the Trunk Impairment Scale Used After Stroke: A Systematic Review Using COSMIN Methodology.
item.description: A systematic review using COSMIN methodology found that the Trunk Impairment Scale (TIS) has clear and certain measurement properties for individuals with stroke, supporting its continued use in rehabilitation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: APTA Report Finds Demand for Pelvic Health Physical Therapy Outpaces Access | APTA
item.description: A new APTA report, "APTA State of Pelvic Health Physical Therapy," reveals that the demand for pelvic health physical therapy in the U.S. significantly outpaces access, highlighting challenges in workforce, access, and awareness.
item.author: APTA

item.title: Pioneering programme launched to test breakthrough spinal treatment in UK rehab centres - Spinal Surgery News
item.description: The UK has launched a pioneering program to test the ARC-EX system, the first dedicated treatment for paralysis approved by UK regulators, which combines targeted electrical spinal cord stimulation with rehabilitation exercises to restore strength and movement in spinal cord injury patients.
item.author: Spinal Surgery News

item.title: Do blood flow restriction exercises offer additional benefits when compared to conventional exercises in musculoskeletal rehabilitation? A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis found that low-load exercise with blood flow restriction (LLE-BFR) offers additional benefits over low-load exercise (LLE) in improving muscle strength, disability, and pain intensity in individuals with musculoskeletal conditions. The review included randomized clinical trials.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Frontline agreement in diagnosis and treatment for musculoskeletal dysfunctions. A prospective, randomized, controlled study.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial demonstrated high agreement between physiotherapists, resident physicians, and senior physicians in diagnosing, treating, and discharging musculoskeletal conditions in an emergency department setting. This supports the implementation of Advanced Physiotherapy Practice roles.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Improved Flexor Spasticity in Patients with Chronic Stroke Using a Dynamic Wrist-Hand Stretching Device: A Pilot Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A pilot RCT (n=30) found that a novel dynamic wrist-hand stretching device, Hand Stretcher®, significantly improved flexor spasticity and hand function in patients with chronic stroke after 4 weeks of use.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Establishing weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury: a systematic review and methodological quality analysis.
item.description: This systematic review identified and analyzed weighted criteria for return-to-play after hamstring injury, highlighting the lack of clear consensus and the high recurrence rate of such injuries.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Multi-Domain Benefits of Exergaming for Sarcopenia Management in Institutionalized Older Adults: Physical, Psychological, and Social Outcomes.
item.description: A pragmatic quasi-experimental study of 72 institutionalized older adults with sarcopenia found a 12-week exergaming intervention (Nintendo Switch Ring Fit Adventure) significantly improved physical, psychological, and social outcomes.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: [Correspondence] Make rehabilitation count: invest in people, not just devices
item.description: This correspondence emphasizes the need to invest in people, not just devices, for rehabilitation, highlighting two persistent gaps in current dialogue that risk undermining progress towards access to rehabilitation for all, following the 79th World Health Assembly review of Rehabilitation 2030.
item.author: The Lancet

item.title: The psychosocial impacts of having eating and drinking difficulties after stroke: a qualitative evidence synthesis.
item.description: A thematic synthesis of 22 studies (n=645 informants) revealed profound psychological and social impacts of eating and drinking difficulties after stroke, extending beyond physical impairment to affect emotional well-being and social participation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Sarcopenia Increases Adjacent Segment Degeneration Risk within 3 Years of Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion.
item.description: An exploratory retrospective cohort study (n=333) found that sarcopenia increased the risk of adjacent segment degeneration within 3 years of anterior cervical discectomy and fusion (ACDF).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Shortwave diathermy for managing knee osteoarthritis: A systematic review with meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review with meta-analysis (17 trials, n=1,372 participants) found that shortwave diathermy significantly improved pain (mean -2.13; 95% CI [-2.64, -1.62]; p < .01) and walking ability (mean -0.72; 95% CI [-1.02, -0.42]; p < .01) in patients with knee osteoarthritis.
item.author: PubMed

item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis (n=NA) evaluated the impact of postpartum exercise on pelvic floor disorders and diastasis recti abdominis, providing evidence for its efficacy in managing these conditions.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Physical therapy protocols for patients with a megaprosthesis after bone tumor resection.
item.description: This study developed evidence-based physical therapy protocols for patients with megaprostheses after bone tumor resection through an explorative literature search and a two-stage consensus procedure involving orthopedic oncologic surgeons and physical therapists.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: In safe hands - patients with knee and hip osteoarthritis expectations and experience of physical therapist-led triage in a secondary care setting.
item.description: This qualitative study explored the expectations and experiences of patients with hip or knee osteoarthritis regarding physical therapist-led triage in a Swedish orthopedic clinic, identifying areas for improvement in shared decision-making.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Impact of heat exposure on muscle-tendon properties and mechanics (PhD Academy Award)
item.description: This PhD research explored the responses of the neuromuscular and musculotendinous system to different heat stress conditions in vivo, focusing on the gastrocnemius medialis muscle-tendon unit.
item.author: BJSM
